title: armeria pseudarmeria subsp. longifolia
intro: Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. longifolia is a subspecies of the Armeria pseudarmeria plant, commonly known as a type of thorny everlasting or false thyme, a flowering perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. longifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable herb for traditional medicine. Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. longifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
